mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, increasing corporate profits and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative forward returns follow INDPRO increases in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market weakness and impending recession, causing investors to discount lower earnings and pushing S&P 500 returns negative over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of cases, or the average drawdown is less than 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales combined with decelerating industrial production creates a demand-supply imbalance that erodes profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The spread between RSAFS and INDPRO growth rates fails to show a negative correlation with forward returns (|Rank IC| < 0.02) over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (unemployment below 4.0%), robust payroll growth fuels wage inflation expectations and imminent Fed tightening, which depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In the tight labor market regime, forward 20-day returns following strong PAYEMS growth are positive in more than 50% of months, or the negative signal fails to outperform a random coin flip.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline of more than 10% in housing starts reflects collapsing residential investment demand, a leading recession indicator that drives S&P 500 returns negative over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines >10% MoM, forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of cases, or the average return is not statistically different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding a 2.5% year-over-year threshold triggers bond selloffs and expectations of aggressive Fed tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%, forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average negative return is less than -0.2%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in real GDP signals broad economic growth that lifts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth below 2% annualized is followed by positive 60-day returns more than half the time, or the rank correlation between GDP growth and forward returns is below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is low (≤2.0%) and industrial production growth is strong (>0.5% month-over-month), the economy risks overheating, prompting future tightening that depresses equity markets over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not statistically negative (p > 0.10).
